title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu unveils a $4.9 billion budget proposal for fiscal year 2027 with a 2.1% spending increase.
The budget faces a $48.4 million deficit due to rising costs, including police overtime and health insurance.
Wu's budget plan addresses reduced federal funding through cutbacks on discretionary grants and delayed hiring.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots Hall of Fame finalists include Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri.
This is Gronkowski's first nomination; he holds the Patriots record for total touchdowns with 80.
Vinatieri holds the NFL record for most points scored and played on New England’s first three Super Bowl teams.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed top prospect James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract to strengthen their playoff roster.
Hagens, 19, scored four points in six games with Providence after finishing his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens's skills could improve Boston's power play and third line as they approach the Stanley Cup Playoffs.

title: Sen. Markey, local clergy call for end to Iran war and Trump’s time in office
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and Boston clergy called for ending U.S. involvement in the Iran war and President Trump's removal.
Markey condemned Trump's rhetoric towards Iran, describing it as dangerous and unfit for someone with nuclear authority.
Faith leaders and activists urged Congress to halt military funding and prioritize diplomacy over conflict and threat.

title: Readers say their electric bills are ‘outrageously high.’ Here’s how much they pay.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: 239 Boston.com readers participated in a poll about electric bill costs; 200 found their bills unmanageable.
Many respondents cited a rise in electric bills, with some reporting increases up to 75% over the last year.
Massachusetts residents with access to municipal utilities reported lower bills compared to those using investor-owned companies.

title: A Nordic-inspired sauna just opened in the White Mountains
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: White Mountain Sauna Haus opened in January in North Conway, N.H., offering Nordic-inspired traditional saunas and cold plunge pools.
The facility features two Finnish saunas, three plunge pools, a cafe, and a community fire pit for relaxation and socializing.
Guests have unrestricted access for 1-2 hours, with amenities like showers, storage cubbies, and complimentary flip flops available.

title: What Patriots coach Mike Vrabel said about viral photos with NFL reporter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini dismissed viral photos of them at an Arizona resort as innocent and misleading.
Vrabel and Russini were seen holding hands and in a hot tub; both stated interactions were public and with a group.
The Athletic's executive editor and Patriots VP of communications supported the claim that the photos lacked context.

title: The perfect spring weekend starts with daffodils
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Daffodils are blooming in New England; visit fields in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and Connecticut this spring.
JetBlue increased checked baggage fees by $4-$9 depending on travel periods for economy passengers.
Tenney Mountain, N.H. offers $20 lift tickets and $10 tubing tickets before closing on April 12.

title: Matt Damon and Noah Kahan will join forces on ‘SNL’ in May
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Matt Damon will host 'Saturday Night Live' on May 9, 2026, his third time hosting the show.
Noah Kahan will perform as the musical guest on 'SNL' on May 9, promoting his album 'The Great Divide'.
Olivia Rodrigo and Will Ferrell will also host 'SNL' in May, with Rodrigo and Paul McCartney as musical guests.

title: Land court backs Somerville church’s plan for homeless shelter that raised neighborhood concerns
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Land Court ruled that First Church Somerville can build a 26-bed homeless shelter under the Dover Amendment.
Neighbors opposed the shelter due to concerns about its 'low-barrier' status affecting neighborhood safety.
The Somerville Homeless Coalition will operate the shelter, offering increased services in response to rising homelessness.
